ogTitle: Potts Point vs Pottsville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Potts Point and Pottsville.
heading: Potts Point vs Pottsville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Potts Point, New South Wales and Pottsville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Potts Point has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Pottsville (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Potts Point or Pottsville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Potts Point has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Pottsville scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Potts Point scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Pottsville scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Potts Point or Pottsville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Potts Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
